---
title: "Claude Opus 4.7's New Tokenizer: ~40% More Tokens Than 4.6"
description: "Simon Willison's Claude Token Counter shows Opus 4.7's new tokenizer maps the same input to ~1.46× more tokens than 4.6, raising effective cost ~40% at unchanged per-token pricing."
author: synesis
date: "2026-04-20"
categories: [Claude, Anthropic, LLMs, links]
image: cover.jpg
---

![Claude Token Counter card from Simon Willison's blog post. Image from [Simon Willison's Weblog](https://simonwillison.net/2026/Apr/20/claude-token-counts/).](cover.jpg){fig-align="center"}

[**Claude Token Counter, now with model comparisons**](https://simonwillison.net/2026/Apr/20/claude-token-counts/) — Simon Willison upgraded his token counter to compare Claude models head-to-head. Headline findings:

- **Opus 4.7 ships a new tokenizer** — the first Claude model to change it. Anthropic's announcement framed it as a 1.0–1.35× token-count increase depending on content type.
- **Real measurement: 1.46×.** Pasting the Opus 4.7 system prompt itself through both tokenizers gave 4.7 ~46% more tokens than 4.6, slightly above Anthropic's stated upper bound.
- **Per-token pricing is unchanged** ($5 / M input, $25 / M output for Opus). So at the same workload, Opus 4.7 effectively costs **~40% more** than Opus 4.6.
- **Images are mostly a wash, with a caveat.** Opus 4.7 also added higher-resolution image support (up to 2,576 px on the long edge — ~3.75 MP, 3× prior models). A 3.7 MB PNG showed a 3.01× token increase, but that was the new resolution support, not the tokenizer; a smaller 682×318 image cost roughly the same on both versions.
- **PDFs land in between.** A 15 MB, 30-page text-heavy PDF showed only a 1.08× multiplier — much lower than raw text alone.
